business.headline: Sensex ends 510 points higher, Nifty tops 23,100; IT stocks lead rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex added 509.73 points to close at 74,616.58, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 155.40 points to end at 23,123.65.

Air India CEO Campbell Wilson confirms exit, says 'time is right' to step downThe airline said Wilson had informed Chairman N Chandrasekaran about his plans to step down in 2026 as early as 2024, and has since been preparing the organisation for a smooth transition.

Oracle brings in new CFO with big pay after recent layoffsAccording to Oracle's official filing, Hilary Maxson, the new CFO, will receive an annual base salary of $950,000. In addition, she will be eligible for a performance-based bonus with a target of $2.5 million.

Gold, silver prices to stay volatile. Should you buy or avoid?Gold is expected to trade between Rs 144,000 and Rs 154,000 per 10 gm, while silver could move in the Rs 215,000 to Rs 243,000 per kg range before a breakout or breakdown.

What every Indian must understand before buying a life insurance policyLife Insurance is not merely a financial product; it is a safeguard for the people who depend on you. Understanding its purpose, structure and role in financial planning can make the difference between simply owning a policy and truly protecting your family.

Air India hikes domestic fares. Here's how much extra passengers will have to payAir India fare hike: The increase in fuel surcharges comes amid the West Asia war that has led to an energy crisis pushing oil prices higher.

From oil prices to war zones: Why global risks shape your investments nowAt the centre of the recent turmoil lies the Strait of Hormuz — a narrow maritime corridor that carries nearly 20 million barrels of oil daily, roughly one-fifth of global consumption.

IndiGo, Air India CEO exits: Inside the crisis hitting aviation sectorThe exits of CEOs at Air India and IndiGO are not isolated events. They come as the aviation sector grapples with a mix of domestic setbacks and global shocks, raising questions about whether the industry is entering a reset phase.

A shipping crisis grows as West Asia war lifts marine insurance risksThe West Asia war is rippling through the Strait of Hormuz, where soaring war-risk premiums and shrinking insurance cover are slowing ships and quietly driving the cost of global trade.

RBI policy decision tomorrow: Will your home loan EMI go up?As the RBI gears up to announce its policy tomorrow, borrowers are watching closely. Will this be a relief moment or the start of higher EMIs? Let's find out.

Gold, silver prices turn volatile after brief rally. What investors should knowOn the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold is currently hovering around Rs 1,49,000 to Rs 1,50,000 per 10 grams, while silver is trading near Rs 2,31,000 to Rs 2,33,000 per kg.

Faster withdrawals, less paperwork: How EPFO 3.0 will change your PF accessEPFO 3.0 is essentially a digital upgrade of the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's systems, designed to reduce manual intervention and improve service delivery for millions of subscribers.

Govt doubles states' quota of 5 kg LPG cylinders meant for migrant workersIn an order issued on April 6, the government said the daily quantity of 5 kg free trade LPG (FTL) cylinders available for distribution to migrant labourers will be doubled, based on the average daily number of cylinders supplied on March 2 and 3, 2026.
Sensex, Nifty show resilience despite Trump's Iran threatThe S&P BSE Sensex was down by 232.74 points to​​​​​​​ 73,874.11, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 76.35 points to 22,891.90 as of 10:10 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash today as Iran war tensions rise?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,889.5 at 8:01 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open below Monday's closing level of 22,968.25.

Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns, to stay till successor namedAir India faces fresh uncertainty as CEO Campbell Wilson steps down, with leadership gaps widening and past controversies still casting a shadow over the Tata Group's ambitious turnaround plans.

DA hike delay: Is this normal or something to worry about?For many, a DA hike around this time is almost expected. But with no announcement yet, the delay is raising a simple question, i.e., is this normal, or is something different this time?

Nayara to shut Vadinar refinery from April 9; will fuel supply take a hit?The timing of this shutdown is important. Ongoing tensions in the Middle East, including the US-Iran conflict, have already tightened the availability of oil and gas globally. This has put pressure on imports of crude oil, natural gas and LPG into India.

The end of Inspector Raj: A reform that assumes too muchMoving beyond the Inspector Raj era marks a clear shift, but lasting success will depend on building systems where trust is supported by credible enforcement and consistent compliance on the ground.
Bima Sugam to launch zero-commission insurance from June: What it means for youThe move is expected to make policies more affordable and easier for customers to understand.

entertainment.headline: Janhvi Kapoor says Shikhar Pahariya is her wild, no-rules fun zone Actor Janhvi Kapoor spills the tea on what makes her bond with Shikhar Pahariya stand out. Here's what she shared about their relationship and what's next for the actor.

Paid preview maths decoded: The Dhurandhar 2 model, now followed by Bhooth Bangla Paid previews are fast becoming Bollywood's new opening formula after Dhurandhar 2. With Bhooth Bangla following suit, the strategy is now under the spotlight.

Allu Arjun-Atlee's film: Title reveal on actor's birthday, April 8; 1st poster out The first poster for Allu Arjun and Atlee's upcoming collaboration, AA22xA6, has unveiled a mysterious, otherworldly beast. The official title is set to be revealed on the actor's birthday, April 8.

Kanye West's London show in trouble, offers to meet Jewish community Kanye West has reportedly offered to meet Britain's Jewish community amid backlash over his Wireless Festival performance in London.

Coorgi women are beautiful, I'm married to one: Vijay's moving speech for Rashmika Vijay Deverakonda's speech at the Kodagu reception reflected deep affection for Rashmika Mandanna and her heritage. He talked about his visits to Coorg and his connection with the place.
Rapper Offset shot in Florida; 2 detained as police probe incident Migos rapper Offset remains stable after shooting at Florida hotel valet area.
Disha Vakani's father, Gujarati actor Bhim Vakani, dies at 84 TV actor Disha Vakani's father and renowned playwright Bhim Vakani died at 84 after battling paralysis and pneumonia.

Anupam Kher calls comedian Samay Raina outstanding at 28, shares life lessons Actor Anupam Kher shared a heartfelt note after meeting comedian Samay Raina and his parents at his acting school. He praised Samay's humour, humility, and reflected on life lessons around making mistakes and learning from them.

Dhurandhar 2 targets historic overtake in Karnataka, set to topple Baahubali 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is closing in on a major box office milestone in Karnataka. The film is set to challenge Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's long-standing record as the top non-Kannada grosser.

Salman Khan's million dollar post for Rajpal Yadav after row over awards show remark Salman Khan came out in strong support of colleague Rajpal Yadav after the comedian faced a remark - seen by many as mocking - at a recent awards show.

Dhurandhar beginning of the end for producers: Telugu writer Ravi talks budget gap Filmmaker and writer BVS Ravi compared Telugu films' budgets with Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar, noting key production differences. He said that Dhurandhar's success marks the beginning of the end for producers.

Matka King trailer: Vijay Varma plays ambitious gambler in 1960s Bombay Vijay Varma's upcoming show, Matka King, brings 1960s Bombay's gambling saga to global audiences. It follows an ambitious cotton trader who turns an elite pastime into a nationwide phenomenon.

9 songs in 9 days: Aditya Dhar recalls Shashwat Sachdev's Dhurandhar music blitz Filmmaker Aditya Dhar shared how his home turned into a living studio during the making of Dhurandhar's songs. He praised Shashwat Sachdev's speed, passion and dedication behind the film's chart-topping music.

Tabu revisits old dance class days with Akshay Kumar: He picked me up on a bike Akshay Kumar and Tabu opened up about their long-standing bond during Bhooth Bangla promotions. From bike rides together to his unchanged habits, they shared fond memories from their early days.

Tovino Thomas calls Lokah 2 biggest challenge in his career. Here's why Malayalam actor Tovino Thomas shared details about the challenging Lokah 2 project, highlighting its complexity and scale. The sequel's production is expected to start later this year, following the success of Lokah Chapter 1.

Viral video: Shah Rukh Khan, Sourav Ganguly share warm hug at Eden Gardens Shah Rukh Khan's warm hug with Sourav Ganguly at Eden Gardens has gone viral and is winning hearts online. The superstar attended the IPL 2026 match with Suhana Khan, though it was called off due to rain.

This pic's going in my will: KJo's forever moment with Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway Karan Johar shares an emotional fanboy moment after meeting Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway in Tokyo. His post, as the duo promotes The Devil Wears Prada 2, has gone viral online.

Chand Mera Dil teaser: Ananya, Lakshya's film offers campus love and heartbreak The Chand Mera Dil teaser showcases Ananya Panday and Lakshya's intense romance, hinting at a story of love, loss and second chances.

Today's fiction quota? Trisha hits back at retirement rumours with witty post Rumours have been rife lately on social media that actor Trisha Krishnan is quitting cinema. The actor on Tuesday responded to the speculations with a witty post on Instagram.

general news.headline: Iran's Kharg Island hit in multiple strikes amid stalled truce talks: Local mediaA day after Iran rejected another ceasefire plan of the US to end the month-long war, Iran's strategic oil hub, Kharg Island, was targeted in multiple strikes, the country's semi-official Mehr News reported on Tuesday.

Live: Iran warns of cutting off Gulf's oil supplies for years if Trump hits infrastructureUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran, warning that "power plants and bridges" could be targeted if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening, saying "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one." Trump's abusive threat drew a sharp from Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who warned that Trump's "reckless moves" risk dragging the country into living hell and insisting "you won't gain anything through war crimes." Behind the scenes, Oman has held urgent talks with Iran to ensure smooth flow of transit through war-hit Hormuz, while Pakistan and Egypt have kept diplomatic channels open between Washington and Tehran. At the same time, violence has intensified elsewhere, with Israeli strikes in Beirut killing at least four people and Hezbollah launching retaliatory attacks, escalating the conflict further. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Internet shut, curfew in Manipur after deadly bomb attack ratchets up tensionsTwo children were killed in a bomb attack on their home in the sensitive Moirang valley of Manipur. The government ordered mobile internet shutdown in five districts and clamped a curfew in Bishnupur to keep the peace amid fears of unrest.

2 attackers gunned down after shooting near Israeli consulate in IstanbulAt least two attackers were gunned down and two police officers injured in a shooting outside a building housing the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. No Israeli diplomats were stationed at the consulate for the past two years.

Salman Khan's million dollar post for Rajpal Yadav after row over awards show remarkSalman Khan came out in strong support of colleague Rajpal Yadav after the comedian faced a remark - seen by many as mocking - at a recent awards show.

155 aircraft, abandoned airport: Theories question US rescue op in IranThe massive scale and nature of the operation to rescue two US crew members stranded inside Iran, which involved over 155 aircraft, has raised an armada of theories. Moreover, little clarity on why the US destroyed its own aircraft has only added to the intrigue. Trump's narrative of the sequence of events has left several unanswered questions.

Eat 5-star, do nothing: China's strategy amid US-Iran warWhile missiles criss-cross the Middle East and tensions rise, China is doing something unusual - doing nothing. China has refused to get itself dragged into the Iran conflict. The situation is well encapsulated in the latest cover of The Economist - Never interrupt your enemy when he's making a mistake.

Trump extended Iran deadline from 48 hours to 408 hours: Will he be kind again?Trump has extended his deadline to Iran three times so far and delayed attacks on energy sites. His new deadline ends today. Will he unleash hell on Iran or give it more time to reopen the Strait of Hormuz?

Coorgi women are beautiful, I'm married to one: Vijay's moving speech for RashmikaVijay Deverakonda's speech at the Kodagu reception reflected deep affection for Rashmika Mandanna and her heritage. He talked about his visits to Coorg and his connection with the place.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Was Trump scammed? US sent guns for Iran protesters, but none firedThe US tried to arm Iranians for regime change through Kurdish channels, President Donald Trump revealed. But none of the guns were ever fired. Did the arms reach the Iranians? Was Trump scammed?

India now has a nuclear reactor that makes more fuel than it burns. Here's howIndia's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam has attained a historic milestone. The 500 MWe reactor, which produces more fuel than it consumes, puts India on course to harness its vast thorium reserves.

This means everything: Aditya Dhar reacts to Virat-Anushka's Dhurandhar 2 reviewAditya Dhar reacted to glowing praise from Virat Kohli and Anushka Sharma for Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The filmmaker called the moment "truly overwhelming" as the film continues its record-breaking run.

Don't use train for next 12 hours for sake of your security, Israel warns IraniansA late-night warning from Israel has raised fresh concerns, asking people across Iran to avoid trains and railway lines, hinting at potential risks as tensions in the region continue to escalate.

Tejas back in the skies. Did HAL waste years and crores to deliver a dud?LCA Tejas fleets are set to return to the skies after a two-month pause following a rude landing in February. But with decades of delays, cost overruns and repeated setbacks for the LCA Tejas, has Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) spent years and thousands of crores on a dud?

Mojtaba Khamenei unconscious, not involved in decision-making: ReportThe Times report said that Mojtaba Khamenei sustained injuries in the same US-Israeli attacks that killed Ayatollah Ali Khameni and is presently undergoing treatment in a private hospital in the city of Qom.

Ever wondered why you get goosebumps? There is hidden science behind itIn this week's edition of The Daily Why, find out why you can feel a chill at the back of your neck or the tiny hair on your arms stand up when you feel cold or scared or excited.

Assam cops at Pawan Khera's home after passport charge against Himanta Sarma's wifeA political row over passport allegations intensifies as Assam Police arrives at Pawan Khera's Delhi residence, with sharp exchanges, legal action and serious claims adding to tensions ahead of crucial polls.

Meet Mriganka Sur, the IIT Kanpur engineer called Newton of brain science at MITFrom IIT Kanpur to MIT, Professor Mriganka Sur's journey spans electrical engineering to pioneering neuroscience, driven by a quest to understand intelligence and the human brain. Now the Newton Professor of Neuroscience at MIT, his research bridges AI, computation, and biology, helping decode how the brain learns and adapts.

Vande Bharat passenger finds insect in meal, IRCTC fines vendor Rs 10 lakhIRCTC fined a catering provider Rs 10 lakh after a passenger found an insect in food on a Vande Bharat train, sparking fresh concerns over onboard hygiene.

Video: Iranian musician performs outside power plant after Trump threatens attackThe artist, Ali Ghamsari, played outside the Damavand Power Plant, a site that the US has threatened to attack if Iran refuses to open Strait of Hormuz by the end of Trump's deadline.

2 children dead, parents critical in Ahmedabad after eating dosaAccording to Gaurishankar Prajapti, the grandfather of the two deceased girls, his son Vimal bought the khiru from a nearby shop named Ghanashyam Dairy to make dosa. The next morning, Vimal, his wife Bhavna, and two of his daughters started vomiting and were taken to the hospital as their conditions worsened.

Human chains, kids as fighters, 14 million volunteers: How Iran plans to fight USIran unveils extensive volunteer and youth mobilisation for defence against US threat. This includes human chains and using children as young as 12 in war.

Career FOMO: Gen Z's new anxiety of watching others' careers, doubting their ownIn a digital-first world where careers are constantly on display, Gen Z is navigating a new kind of pressure: Career FOMO. As achievements become content and success becomes visible, the line between inspiration and anxiety continues to blur, reshaping how young professionals perceive growth, ambition and self-worth.

sports.headline: Warner arrested for drink-driving in Sydney. Will it affect Pak league stint? Former Australian opener David Warner was arrested for drink-driving in Sydney on Sunday while on a break from his Pakistan Super League duties. Warner has been leading the Karachi Kings in the 11th edition of the PSL.

Jannik Sinner admits missing Carlos Alcaraz rivalry, hopes for Monte Carlo showdown Monte Carlo Masters: Jannik Sinner said he misses facing Carlos Alcaraz and their rivalry, adding that he enjoys their battles because the Spaniard pushes him to his limits.

IPL sacrifices made me lose my England career: Pietersen opens up on ECB fallout Kevin Pietersen said his dispute with the England and Wales Cricket Board over playing in the Indian Premier League led to the premature end of his international career, calling it a personal sacrifice that later benefited other players. He added that his stance helped pave the way for England cricketers to participate more freely in franchise T20 leagues.

Vaishnavi Adkar crowned India's new tennis No.1 with sensational run in 2026 Vaishnavi Adkar's dream 2026 run has taken her to the top of Indian women's singles, with the 21-year-old climbing from World No. 643 to No.383 to become the country's new No.1. She is currently taking part in the Billie Jean King Cup 2026 in New Delhi.

Dump ODIs, stick to T20s and IPL: Lalit Modi's bold suggestion sparks buzz Lalit Modi has stirred debate by calling for ODI cricket to be scrapped, arguing the format no longer fits the modern game shaped by T20 leagues and shifting fan interest. His comments come even as attention slowly builds towards the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Shreyas Iyer's sister not holding back, trolls KKR after washout in Kolkata: Watch IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer's sister, Shresta, went viral after cheekily trolling Kolkata Knight Riders with an Instagram reel following the rain-hit washout of their clash against Punjab Kings at Eden Gardens.

Hetmyer's form not a concern for RR: Coach backs batter to rise from slump IPL 2026: RR have downplayed concerns over Shimron Hetmyer's slow start to IPL 2026, backing the West Indies star to come good soon. Performance coach Siddhartha Lahiri stressed that it is too early to judge, insisting the team remains confident in its middle-order strength.

Tim David reveals RCB drill behind 106m six vs CSK: We compete to hit the roof IPL 2026: Tim David revealed RCB's unique roof-hitting drills behind his 106m six vs CSK. The Aussie powered RCB to a massive total with a stunning late assault.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi storm again? How Jasprit Bumrah, MI can stop RR teen sensation IPL 2026, RR vs MI: Ex-Australia skipper Aaron Finch believes Jasprit Bumrah should target Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early with fuller deliveries and swing when Mumbai Indians face Rajasthan Royals on Tuesday at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium in Guwahati.

Tim David hits 106m six out of Chinnaswamy: But where did the ball disappear? Watch IPL 2026, RCB vs CSK: Tim David lit up IPL 2026 with a monstrous 106-metre six that landed on the Chinnaswamy roof before flying out of the ground during Royal Challengers Bengaluru's clash against Chennai Super Kings on Sunday.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's first dance with Jasprit Bumrah takes spotlight RR vs MI, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faces his biggest challenge yet as he comes up against Jasprit Bumrah for the first time, with the RR youngster set for a defining test in the Guwahati clash against MI.

KKR woes deepen: Pathirana still awaiting IPL clearance from Sri LankaIPL 2026: KKR's bowling woes continue as Matheesha Pathirana awaits IPL clearance. The Sri Lankan pacer could join the squad only by mid-April.

Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hype, Ravi Bishnoi no mysteryRR vs MI, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's hype ahead of RR clash, with Paras Mhambrey saying there are no special plans, while also dismissing Ravi Bishnoi as a mystery threat.

Green not good enough? Pietersen's scathing take on KKR's misfiring starKevin Pietersen delivered a blunt take on Cameron Green after the Australian all-rounder continued to struggle in the IPL 2026 campaign. Green scored just four runs during the match against PBKS.

Graceful in defeat, Sheetal Devi helps limbless archer Payal Nag on podium: VideoSheetal Devi showed rare grace in defeat, helping limbless archer Payal Nag face the flag on the podium. The teenager from Odisha had just stunned the world number one to win gold in the World Archery Para Series in Bangkok.

Heavenly intervention: Rain washes away KKR's toss blunder vs PBKS in KolkataIPL 2026, KKR vs PBKS: Kolkata Knight Riders were reeling at 25 for 2 in the fourth over when rain intervened, forcing a no-result in Kolkata. Ajinkya Rahane's decision to win the toss and opt to bat under overcast skies drew sharp criticism.

Did R Ashwin take a dig at Rahane's call to bat vs Punjab in rainy Kolkata?R Ashwin was surprised by KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane's call to bat first in rainy Kolkata against PBKS at the Eden Gardens on Monday, April 6. Rahane justified his call to bat first, but not everyone was convinced by the decision.

Where is the hype? Cameron Green flops yet again for KKR, fans left frustratedKKR vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Cameron Green's poor run continues as fans question his role and price, with KKR under pressure after back-to-back losses and missing Narine and Chakravarthy in IPL 2026 clash.

KKR without Sunil Narine, Varun Chakravarthy for first time in 7 yearsKKR vs PBKS, IPL 2026 Playing XIs: Kolkata Knight Riders will not have both Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine in their side for the first time in seven years.

Will MI captain Hardik Pandya be ready for RR clash? Bowling coach offers updateMumbai Indians have offered an update on Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder missed the loss against Delhi Capitals. Hardik missed the game due to illness.

PSL to beat IPL? The financial reality behind Mohsin Naqvi's No. 1 league claimPCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi claims the PSL will soon become the most sought-after T20 league in cricket. However, with the IPL's $18.5 billion valuation dwarfing the PSL's $260 million, the financial reality suggests a massive divide.

Is Avesh Khan in trouble? Pacer's antics after Pant's winning runs go viralAvesh Khan faces scrutiny after a boundary-side incident during LSG's win over SRH, adding to the franchise's growing concerns over contentious decisions this IPL season.

How Test retirement unlocked Kohli as a T20 batter in IPL? R Ashwin explainsRavichandran Ashwin explains how Test retirement helped Virat Kohli improve his T20 strike rate with a new batting approach after the RCB vs CSK clash. Kohli scored 28 off 18 balls and gave RCB early momentum in their innings.

Sanju Samson vs Jasprit Bumrah: India's T20 World Cup heroes in hunt for ICC awardSanju Samson and Jasprit Bumrah lead ICC Player of the Month nominees after starring in India's T20 World Cup triumph, while South Africa's Connor Esterhuizen impressed with a breakout series in New Zealand.

KKR vs PBKS Highlights: Rain saves Kolkata's blushes as match called offKolkata (KKR) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: A batting blunder from Kolkata has been saved by rain as the match at the Eden Gardens against Punjab gets called off. Having won the toss, KKR opted to bat first in a bizarre call and were made to pay as they were reeling at 25 for 2 in 3.4 overs. Rain was relentless for a good portion of the night and despite relenting at the end, the match had to be called off. Both teams get one point each in the end.

RCB star gets Dinesh Karthik's backing for India call-up: Difficult to keep him outIPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru mentor Dinesh Karthik heaped praise on Devdutt Padikkal's rich vein of form and leadership qualities, saying the 25-year-old left-hander will push for a place in the Indian T20I side. Padikkal smashed a fifty in RCB's 43-run win over Chennai Super Kings on Sunday.

IPL 2026: Rashid Khan is developing a secret deliveryGujarat Titans spinner Rashid Khan is developing a secret delivery. Speaking to India Today, Rashid revealed that he has been forced to experiment due to the rapid evolution of batters in the last two years in the league.

Candidates: Vaishali emerges as contender, Praggnanandhaa trails SindarovThe Candidates Chess 2026 tournament has reached its halfway stage after seven rounds. While the women's section is heating up thanks to Vaishali's surge, Praggnanandhaa has been outpaced by the brilliance of Javokhir Sindarov in the Open category.

Satwik-Chirag withdraw from Badminton Asia ChampionshipsIndia's Badminton Asia Championships campaign has suffered a major blow with Satwik-Chirag pulling out due to injury. With the star doubles duo injured, the spotlight shifts to Lakshya Sen and PV Sindhu to carry the nation's hopes and end a decades-long wait for a singles title.

Struggling Kolkata face added headache as rain threat looms at Eden GardensIPL 2026, KKR vs PBKS: Kolkata Knight Riders have plenty to worry about, especially with their team combination, as they look to bounce back against Punjab Kings on Monday at Eden Gardens. Meanwhile, there is also a threat of rain, just as when the two teams met last year in Kolkata.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
